title: CHAPTER 5 —
PLANS, APPROVALS, AND REDIRECTION
segments.body_html: A Cowork plan is a proposal, not a commitment. Review it before any action starts. Good plan review checks five things:

beats.text: A Cowork plan is a proposal, not a commitment. Review it before any action starts. Good plan review checks five things: goal fit, source coverage, risky steps, missing verification, and stop conditions. You have four responses to a plan: approve, redirect, pause, or stop. All four are legitimate. Clicking approve without understanding the plan is not supervision. It is abdication with extra steps.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: OPENING SCENE
segments.body_html:
·Inventory all files in the folder.
·Identify duplicates and outdated drafts.
·Propose a naming convention.
·Apply the convention to all files.
·Remove duplicates and outdated drafts.

beats.text: The plan looks competent. Cowork has laid out six steps for organizing a grant files folder: Inventory all files in the folder. Identify duplicates and outdated drafts. Propose a naming convention. Apply the convention to all files. Remove duplicates and outdated drafts. Produce a final folder manifest.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · OPENING SCENE

segments.section: A PLAN IS A PROPOSAL, NOT PROO
title: A PLAN IS A
PROPOSAL, NOT PROOF.
segments.body_html:
Agentic AI systems, including Cowork, can produce plans that are internally coherent but brittle — plans that handle the

A Plan Is a Proposal, Not Proof — This matches a well-established principle from automation research: humans must remain engaged enough to intervene (Bain

beats.text: Agentic AI systems, including Cowork, can produce plans that are internally coherent but brittle , plans that handle the expected case well and the unexpected case badly (Liang et al. 2024; "Revealing the Barriers," arXiv 2024). Fluent language is not evidence of correct reasoning about your specific files, your specific constraints, or the specific risks in your workspace. This matches a well-established principle from automation research: humans must remain engaged enough to intervene (Bainbridge 1983).

segments.label: 03 / 13 · A PLAN IS A PROPOSAL, NOT PROO

title: THE PLAN-REVIEW
CHECKLIST.
segments.body_html:
·Does the plan match the objective in my brief?
·Are there steps that are not connected to the objective?
·Has Cowork made assumptions about scope, audience, or priority that I did not specify?
·Are the source files or folders named or inventoried in the plan?
·Does the plan respect the allowed sources in my brief?

beats.text: Before approving any Cowork plan, work through these questions: Goal fit Does the plan match the objective in my brief? Are there steps that are not connected to the objective? Has Cowork made assumptions about scope, audience, or priority that I did not specify?

segments.label: 04 / 13 · THE PLAN-REVIEW CHECKLIST

title: FOUR RESPONSES TO
A PLAN.
segments.body_html: Approve.→Redirect.→Pause.→Stop.

beats.text: The approval step has more options than approve or reject. Approve. The plan is bounded, matches the brief, separates risky steps from reversible steps, and includes verification. Proceed.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · FOUR RESPONSES TO A PLAN

title: WORKED WALKTHROUGH —
REPORT PLAN REVIEW
segments.body_html:
Goal fityes — all steps connect to the board summary objective.
Source coveragestep 1 mentions the correct folder; the exclusions (draft files, template files) are not mentioned. Problem.
Risky stepsno file moves, no deletions, no external submission. Writing a new file is low-risk. Acceptable.
Missing verificationthere is no step where Priya sees the source inventory and proposed structure before the draft is written. Problem.
Stop conditionsno mention of what happens if financial figures are absent. Problem.

beats.text: Priya submits her task brief from Chapter 4 for the board summary. Cowork responds with this plan: 1. Read and inventory files in /tasks/board-summary-june. 2. Identify Q3 and Q4 2024 program result sections. 3. Draft the executive summary. 4. Draft section-by-section results by program area. 5. Produce board-summary-june-2024.docx.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · WORKED WALKTHROUGH — REPORT PL

title: COMPUTER USE AND
HIGH-SUPERVISION PLANS
segments.body_html:
·Require each step to be shown before it is executed (Anthropic, "Let Claude use your computer in Cowork" ).
·Explicitly prohibit form submissions, purchases, and account actions unless each is pre-approved.
·Name the specific sites or applications Cowork is permitted to interact with.
·Keep sensitive windows closed and unrelated applications hidden during the session.

beats.text: When a plan includes browser access or computer use, plan review requires more attention, not less. Cowork interacting with a browser or desktop application can move through multiple steps quickly, and each step may have consequences , page navigation, form input, account interaction , that are not easily reversible. Require each step to be shown before it is executed (Anthropic, "Let Claude use your computer in Cowork" ). Explicitly prohibit form submissions, purchases, and account actions unless each is pre-approved.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · COMPUTER USE AND HIGH-SUPERVIS

segments.section: THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTE
title: THE HUMAN GATE FOR
THIS CHAPTER.
segments.body_html:
Plan review is where your knowledge of the work — knowledge Cowork cannot have — enters the process before any action is

The Human Gate for This Chapter — The plan review checklist gives your knowledge a place to land. Working through the checklist is not bureaucratic cautio

beats.text: Plan review is where your knowledge of the work , knowledge Cowork cannot have , enters the process before any action is taken. You know which files are sensitive. You know which program results are contested. You know that the "duplicates" folder actually contains earlier application versions that might matter. Cowork does not. The plan review checklist gives your knowledge a place to land. Working through the checklist is not bureaucratic caution. It is the act of supervision that makes delegation responsible.

segments.label: 09 / 13 · THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTE

title: COMMON MISTAKES
segments.body_html: Approving because the plan sounds reasonable.→Treating redirection as evidence of failure.→Approving once and not monitoring.→Skipping the risky-step check for "simple" tasks.→Not reading the stop conditions.

beats.text: Approving because the plan sounds reasonable. Reasonable prose is not the same as a safe plan. Check the specific steps, not the overall impression. Treating redirection as evidence of failure. Redirection is the review process working. A plan that needed no revision was either trivial or was not checked carefully.

segments.label: 10 / 13 · COMMON MISTAKES

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html: Lucy Suchman→Run this:→"Lucy Suchman plans and situated action"→Now make the prompt better.

beats.text: Lucy Suchman wrote Plans and Situated Actions in 1987, arguing that plans are not scripts that people follow step by step. They are resources people draw on while doing something that is actually more improvised, contextual, and responsive to what actually happens. A plan and the situated reality rarely match exactly , and the gap between them is where the real work of adjustment and judgment takes place. Cowork's proposed plan is a resource, not a script. Your plan review is the situated judgment.

segments.label: 11 / 13 · AI WAYBACK MACHINE
